thread.title: maharashtra assembly polls: Mahayuti rises from the ashes of a shoddy Lok Sabha show to make a Maha-comeback - The Economic Times
thread.title_full: maharashtra assembly polls: Mahayuti rises from the ashes of a shoddy Lok Sabha show to make a Maha-comeback - The Economic Times

title: maharashtra assembly polls: Mahayuti rises from the ashes of a shoddy Lok Sabha show to make a Maha-comeback - The Economic Times
text: The Mahayuti alliance of BJP, Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, and Ajit Pawar's NCP has recorded a landslide victory in the Maharashtra assembly elections, winning 230 of the state’s 288 seats. This marks an 80% success rate, securing a dominant three-fourths majority. The win comes just six months after the alliance faced setbacks in the Lok Sabha elections, showcasing a remarkable turnaround.The opposition MVA, which led in 153 assembly segments during the Lok Sabha polls, has been reduced to 46 seats. Among the factors credited for the Mahayuti's victory is the Ladki Bahin Yojana, a direct cash transfer scheme for women. Additionally, the consolidation of the OBC and pro-Hindutva votes played a significant role in the alliance’s success.BJP’s Record-Setting PerformanceBJP emerged as the key driver of the victory, winning 132 seats—its highest tally in Maharashtra—surpassing the 122 seats it won in 2014. The victory also bolsters Deputy Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis's standing as a strong contender for the chief ministerial position. The Shiv Sena faction led by Eknath Shinde won 57 seats, while Ajit Pawar's NCP faction secured 41.In contrast, the Congress faced its worst-ever performance in Maharashtra, securing just 16 seats. Uddhav Thackeray's Shiv Sena (UBT) managed only 20 seats, while Sharad Pawar’s NCP faction was reduced to 10.Women-Focused Schemes as GamechangersThe Ladki Bahin Yojana has been hailed as a pivotal factor in Mahayuti's victory. Modeled after Madhya Pradesh's Ladli Behna scheme, it provides a monthly stipend of ₹1,500 to underprivileged women aged 21 to 65 with annual family incomes below ₹2.5 lakh. By election day, over 2.4 crore women had received three installments totaling ₹7,500. The promise to raise the stipend to ₹2,100 further boosted the scheme's appeal.Additional measures targeting women included three free gas cylinders for 52 lakh households and free higher education for girls from families earning below ₹8 lakh annually. These initiatives contributed to a six-percentage-point surge in female voter turnout, rising to 65.2% from 59.2% in 2019.Leaving Shadows of Lok Sabha BehindMahayuti’s comeback reflects strategic responses to issues that impacted its performance in the Lok Sabha elections. In Vidarbha and Marathwada, where farmer distress over crop prices was a concern, the alliance countered with a promise of price support for key crops. The BJP also addressed onion-export grievances by slashing export duties and minimum export prices ahead of the polls.Ajit Pawar's control over western Maharashtra’s cooperative networks, including sugar factories and banks, helped his faction dominate this region. In urban areas like Mumbai, the BJP outperformed its rivals, capturing 15 seats compared to the Shiv Sena (UBT)’s 10 and Eknath Shinde’s 6.The results have significant implications for Maharashtra's political landscape. Eknath Shinde’s faction and Ajit Pawar's NCP have emerged as legitimate heirs to their respective party legacies, overshadowing Uddhav Thackeray and Sharad Pawar. Analysts suggest the verdict signifies an existential crisis for these traditional leaders.Observers note that the MVA's inability to counter the Mahayuti’s strategies, combined with the latter's recognition of growing women voters and targeted schemes, sealed the saffron alliance's sweeping victory.with TOI inputs
